title: Darth Revan's Sith Empire

{{Quote|Three years ago, Revan and Malak returned at the head of a massive invasion fleet. Revan had assumed the title of Sith Lord; the hero had become a conqueror.|[[Dorak]]|<ref name="KOTOR" />}}
'''Darth Revan's Sith Empire''' was a revived faction of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith Order]] founded by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], [[Revan/Legends|Darth Revan]], and his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]], [[Darth Malak]], as a [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] organization in direct opposition to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and its [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] defenders.

Built upon a hardened core of veterans from the [[Mandalorian Wars]], and a host of Jedi converts, this new galactic power very nearly succeeded in conquering the Republic during the [[Jedi Civil War]]. After the redemption of Revan and the death of Malak, the Empire splintered into factions before finally dissolving entirely circa [[3950 BBY]].

==History==
===The founding===
{{Quote|After Malachor, after the Mandalorian Wars, that's when the Sith teachings started spreading through the ranks. We knew where our loyalties lay – to the Jedi who came to help us, not the ones who sat back on Dantooine and Coruscant, watching us die.|Atton Rand|<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Revanludotomb.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Darth Revan]]
The beginnings of Revan's Sith Empire could be found towards the end of the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. [[Revan/Legends|Revan]], the Republic's Jedi commander, visited the ancient [[Trayus Academy]] on the Sith world of [[Malachor V]]. While unearthing the planet's buried secrets, Revan discovered evidence that the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith Empire]] still existed beyond the boundaries of known space and continued to pose a threat to [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

As Revan's forces engaged in battle with the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] above the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Malachor V]], he dueled their leader [[Mandalore the Ultimate]] to the death while the two factions' fleets waited above. In Mandalore's final moments, he informed Revan that he had been influenced by a mysterious faction of [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]. After the Mandalorian fleet experienced a crushing defeat above Malachor and surrendered, Revan and his friend [[Darth Malak|Malak]] traveled to the ice world of [[Rekkiad]] to investigate their fallen foe's claims. From there, they followed a trail that led them into the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]]<ref name="Revan">''[[The Old Republic: Revan]]''</ref> with almost a third of the Republic fleet—justifying their departure by claiming to be pursuing the remaining Mandalorian forces.<ref name="KOTOR" />

However, Revan and Malak eventually [[Mission to Dromund Kaas|found their way]] to [[Dromund Kaas/Legends|Dromund Kaas]] and discovered that the Sith Empire indeed still lived. Their attempt to eliminate the [[Tenebrae|Sith Emperor]] went horribly wrong, with the Emperor dominating their minds and finally turning them to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]. Programming them to attack the Republic in order to test the enemy's weaknesses,<ref name="Revan" /> the Emperor named Revan [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] and bestowed the Darth title on them.<ref name="TOR:TF">{{TORcite|type=flashpoint|The Foundry}}</ref> The Emperor then tasked them to use their knowledge of the forgotten [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] [[Infinite Empire/Legends|Infinite Empire]] to find the legendary [[Star Forge]], which would help him create an armada that would crush the Republic.<ref name="Timeline 8">{{TORweb|url=holonet/galactic-history|text=Timeline 8: The Jedi Civil War|int=Timeline 8: The Jedi Civil War}}</ref> The next year saw them secretly search for ancient [[Star Map]]s which would lead them to the Station.<ref name="Encyclopedia-">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia]]''</ref>

Revan was to prepare the [[Great Galactic War|major offensive]] against the Republic by the order of the Emperor. However, the Emperor underestimated the two former Jedi's resolve—Revan and Malak broke free of their conditioning and began to act of their own will.<ref name="Revan" /> From the deck of the Star Forge, hidden in the unmapped [[Lehon system]], Revan founded a new Sith Empire. Adapting the [[Sith sorcery/Legends|teachings]] of the ancient Sith to his own needs, he then took his friend Malak as his Sith apprentice.<ref name="KOTOR" />

A year after their apparent disappearance, Revan and Malak returned at the head of a massive invasion fleet made up of Republic vessels such as the [[Leviathan (Interdictor-class cruiser)|''Leviathan'']], as well as strange hybrid technologies built by the Star Forge. They were crewed by experienced ex-Republic veterans, and commanded by some of the finest officers that the Republic had produced including [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Saul Karath]],<ref name="KOTOR" /> [[General/Legends|General]] [[Derred]], and [[Mon Halan]].<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" /> Calling themselves the Sith, they declared war on the Republic.<ref name="KOTOR" />

===The Jedi Civil War===
{{Main|Jedi Civil War}}
[[File:Malak_kotorpromo.jpg|thumb|right|175px|Darth Malak]]
With Darth Revan at the helm, the Sith invasion was all but unstoppable.<ref name="KOTOR" /> His [[Sith assassin/Legends|Sith assassins]] secretly flooded into the Republic from their base on Malachor V, killing and/or abducting Jedi throughout Republic space. On Malachor V, Revan's most faithful servants broke captured Jedi and led them to the dark side.<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" /> The Sith conspired with [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka Corporation]], one of the richest and most powerful galaxy-spanning companies of the time, to amass much needed resources. They also secured a place on the ocean planet of [[Manaan/Legends|Manaan]], thanks to the native [[Selkath/Legends|Selkath]] neutrality, and so were able to export the healing agent [[Kolto/Legends|kolto]] to their forces.<ref name="KOTOR" />

As the Sith's conquest continued unchecked, worlds began to secede to the Sith invasion fleet voluntarily, either out of fear of what had happened to [[Telos IV]] or because they believed that the corrupt and ineffectual Republic was finally about to fall. The Republic, however, still had one last card to play—the Jedi. Time and again, the Republic armadas were saved from certain destruction by the prowess of a young Jedi [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] named [[Bastila Shan]]. During a long string of Republic losses, her skills in [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]] staved off utter defeat.<ref name="KOTOR" />

When the Sith were on the brink of victory, [[Darth Revan's flagship|Revan's flagship]] was invaded by a Jedi strike team during an ambush. Led by Bastila, the Jedi were able to corner Revan on the bridge. Ever ambitious, Malak fired upon his master's vessel, hoping to destroy all of his enemies with one swift stroke, but all did not go as he had planned. The ship was destroyed, but the Jedi were able to escape with a critically injured Revan.<ref name="KOTOR" />

====The betrayal of Darth Malak====
After the apparent death of Revan, Malak crowned himself as the new [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] and continued his former master's war of conquest, but he did not share Revan's vision. Afterwards, he took his own apprentice, [[Darth Bandon]]. Whereas Revan had kept key Republic infrastructure intact for the purposes of post-war rebuilding, Malak and Bandon preferred an all-out war of destruction and carnage.<ref name="KOTOR" />

[[File:Taris_surf.jpg|thumb|thumb|left|250px|The ''[[Endar Spire]]'' above [[Taris/Legends|Taris]].]]

Malak's primary objective quickly became the assassination or capture of Bastila Shan. He almost got his wish when Bandon overwhelmed and boarded her [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the ''[[Endar Spire]]'', during a Sith ambush over the world of [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]. Bastila managed to flee the battle in an [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]]. She was soon joined by an amnesiac Revan who had escaped the ''Endar Spire'' with the aid of Saul Karath's old protégé [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Carth Onasi]]. After a fruitless quarantine and search of the planet, Darth Malak ordered Admiral Karath to destroy it. The entire surface of Taris—including all life there—was utterly obliterated during a combined sterilization operation by the Sith fleet, but Bastila, Revan and their party managed to escape and flee to the [[Jedi Enclave]] on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]] onboard a stolen smuggler vessel named the ''[[Ebon Hawk]]''.<ref name="KOTOR" />

[[File:Starmap2.jpg|thumb|right|125px|A [[Star Map]].]]

On Dantooine, the unaware Revan was retrained in the ways of the Force before given a quest of vital importance to the Republic—retracing Darth Revan's path to the Star Forge. After obtaining data from three damaged Rakatan Star Maps and with the death of Bandon, the crew of the ''Ebon Hawk'' were captured by Saul Karath's command ship, the [[Leviathan (Interdictor-class cruiser)|''Leviathan'']]. Fortunately, Bastila and her party managed to escape from their cells and confronted Saul Karath on the bridge, where he was killed. On the verge of escaping the vessel, Darth Malak arrived and faced them. During the confrontation, Revan's true identity was revealed and Bastila sacrificed herself so that he and the others could escape. She was captured by Malak and after being tortured, she fell to the dark side and became his new apprentice.<ref name="KOTOR" />

====The fall====
{{Quote|Malak had strength, and embraced it, saw his opportunity and took it. Then Lord Revan had the chance to rule over an empire, to heal this broken galaxy and make it strong again – instead he chose to surrender it to weakness, and he ran away.|[[Darth Sion]]|<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />}}
[[File:Sf-battle.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The [[Battle of Rakata Prime]].]]

Having discovered the ancient Star Maps on Dantooine, [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]], Manaan, and [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], Revan and his crew were able to piece together the location of the Star Forge. The Republic armadas were summoned to [[Lehon/Legends|Lehon]] and a massive [[Battle of Rakata Prime|space battle]] took place between the Republic and Sith forces. After a confrontation with Revan, Bastila returned to the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side]] and betrayed her master by using her battle meditation to aid the Republic forces. The [[Jedi Civil War]] ended as Darth Malak died at the hands of his old master. With Malak gone and the Star Forge destroyed, the infrastructure of the Sith Empire collapsed.<ref name="KOTOR" /> The remaining [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] descended into a state of [[Sith Civil War|civil war]] and virtually wiped themselves out.<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />

Small skirmishes led by ex-Sith forces continued for the next few years, until much of what Revan had built was undone. In time, three new Sith Lords soon arose to take control of a remnant of the Empire, but they were never strong enough to directly challenge the Republic. [[Sith Triumvirate|The fragile alliance]] between [[Darth Nihilus]], [[Darth Sion]], and [[Kreia|Darth Traya]] which almost completely decimated the Jedi was eventually destroyed by [[Meetra Surik]],<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" /> bringing an end to the Sith legacy that Revan had begun.<ref name="Revan" /> Revan, meanwhile, had disappeared from known space, apparently continuing his previous tactics to take on the ancient Sith Empire by himself.<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" /> Meetra followed him and eventually got herself killed while Revan was imprisoned in stasis<ref name="Revan" /> for centuries. [[3681 BBY|Nearly three hundred years later]], the ancient Sith Empire would [[Great Galactic War|return]] to plague the Republic once more.<ref name="TOR">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''</ref>

==Government and politics==
During the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], disciples of [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] established a [[Sith Academy (Korriban)|Sith Academy]] on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> Kun's disciples maintained this academy after Kun's defeat.<ref name="sal">{{Tales|23|Shadows and Light}}</ref> When Revan and Malak became Dark Lords of the Sith, Kun's disciples swore allegiance to them and Korriban became the public capital of their empire.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide" /> Korriban thus began to attract ever-growing numbers of students and acolytes to the burgeoning [[Sith Academy (Korriban)|Sith Academy]] there.<ref name="KOTOR" /> 

[[File:Korribanlandscape.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]].]]

The organization of Darth Revan's Sith Empire was surprisingly similar to that of [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]]' [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. The guiding principle of the Empire was natural selection or survival of the fittest. Even the lowliest grunts were encouraged to compete for positions of power through any means necessary. While this means of promotion created a tense and divisive system of governance, it nevertheless ensured a powerful and capable military. Revan, then Malak after him, were the supreme rulers of the Empire and their strength and charisma alone kept their minions in check.<ref name="KOTOR" />

The Sith had a unique way of managing conquered territories. A single governor, often a [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]], would be posted on each world and housed within an embassy which would contain a security force large enough to keep the planet under control. The Sith fleet was often on the move which allowed even the Dark Lord of the Sith to command from the front lines. All of these structures were designed to expedite the support and deployment of the Empire's military forces, rather than to provide a sedate governing body such as the Galactic Republic.<ref name="KOTOR" />

The Sith Academy on Korriban followed similar rules. Prospective students learned to care only for themselves and to be ruthlessly independent. Additionally, the [[Sith_Academy_%28Korriban%29#Headmasters|Master]] of the [[Sith Academy (Korriban)|academy]] could, at any time, be succeeded by his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]].<ref name="KOTOR" />

==Military==
[[File:Sithfleetkotor2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] Navy amassing at the [[Star Forge]].]]
{{Main|Sith military (Jedi Civil War)}}

Upon their return to known space, the Sith were well equipped for battle. The new Sith Empire's fleets were primarily dominated by stolen Republic vessels during the early stages of the Jedi Civil War, but they were soon supplemented by a host of starships built by the Star Forge. The Star Forge itself served as a primary staging ground and command center.<ref name="KOTOR" />

===Navy===
*[[Interdictor-class cruiser|''Interdictor''-class cruisers]] such as the [[Leviathan (Interdictor-class cruiser)|''Leviathan'']] were among the finest vessels of the time period.
*[[Centurion-class battlecruiser/Legends|''Centurion''-class battlecruisers]] exemplified by the [[Ravager (Centurion-class)|''Ravager'']].
*[[Derriphan-class battleship|''Derriphan''-class battleships]]
*[[Sith fighter]]s were a hybrid of Republic and Rakatan technologies mass produced by the Star Forge.
*[[KT-400 military droid carrier]]s
*[[Herald-class shuttle|''Herald''-class shuttles]]
*Sith Bombers

===Army===
[[File:Sith-empire-ranks.jpg|thumb|right|250px|From left to right: A Sith trooper, Sith Officer, Sith Assassin, and Dark Jedi.]]
*[[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] was the leader.
*[[Shadow Hand]] was the second-in-command of the Dark Lord.
*[[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]] were the leading force of the Sith right under the Dark Lord.
*[[Sith acolyte/Legends|Sith Acolytes]] and [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentices]] were the trainees of the Lords and were mainly stationed on the [[Star Forge]] and the [[Temple of the Ancients]].
*[[Sith officer]]s were the commanders of the Sith's ground and space forces.
*[[Sith trooper (Sith Empire)|Sith troopers]] served as the backbone and main fighting force of the Sith military.
*[[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] were Jedi that had fallen to the dark side and supported the Sith's main forces.
*[[Sith assassin/Legends|Sith assassins]] were used exclusively to hunt, capture, or kill Jedi.
*Various forms of [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] were mass produced by the Star Forge for attack and defense purposes. Known variants included the following:
**[[Sentinel droid]]s were known battle droid series used by the Sith Empire.
**[[Mark IV assault droid]]s were quadrupedal battle droids which were often used to guard their military bases.

==Astrography==
The objective of the Sith Empire of Darth Revan was to seize territory and ultimately replace the Republic, unlike the Sith of the Great Sith War, whose primary goal was to sow discord and chaos. While the Sith of old devastated half of the settled galaxy, Revan's Sith Empire at its height had seized control of a third of the known galaxy, and, just prior to the defeat at the Battle of Rakata Prime, higher-ranking Sith officials estimated that it would only be a matter of months before the Sith encroached upon the inner [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] themselves, with the Republic's fall projected shortly thereafter.

[[File:LehonBeach.jpg|thumb|left|[[Lehon/Legends|Lehon]].]]

During its height, the Empire governed a large swath of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]], including much of what was formerly known as [[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith Space]]. Several islands of territory existed beyond the mainstream Sith Empire, including those based upon the Corellian and Tapani Sectors, giving the Sith Empire a strong presence in the Core Worlds.

==Behind the scenes==
The canonical outcome of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' is that Revan was redeemed to the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side of the Force]] and aided the Republic in turning the tide against the Sith Empire. However, the game also contains a non-canon dark side ending where he/she took back the mantle of Dark Lord of the Sith from his treacherous former apprentice Darth Malak.<ref name="KOTOR" />

If the player of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords|Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' selects this ending (during the first conversation with [[Atton Rand]]), a recording found on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], narrated by [[Bastila Shan|Bastila]], indicates that Darth Revan returned to the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] a couple of years after the [[Battle of Rakata Prime]]. The power vacuum this created led to Revan's empire collapsing in infighting between lesser Sith.<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords" />
